data.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited the victims of the Red Fort blast at Delhi’s Lok Nayak Jai Prakash (LNJP) Hospital on Wednesday, shortly after returning from his two-day trip to Bhutan. The powerful explosion at one of Delhi’s most famous landmarks had killed 10 people and injured over 20. After landing in Delhi, PM Modi […]

data.description: Even though the Tehreek-e-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) took responsibility for the suicide blast in Islamabad that killed 12 people on Tuesday, PM Sharif claimed that “Indian-sponsored terrorist groups” were actually behind the attack.

data.description: The blast occurred in a moving Hyundai i20 car in which three people were sitting. We have not found any pellet or puncture in the body of the injured, which is unusual in a bomb blast.

data.title: Kerala police steps up security following Delhi blasts
data.description: Officials said the State police initiated the sweeping security exercise after preliminary investigation pointed to the possible involvement of terrorist groups; they said the police had initiated a high alert level not seen since the Easter Day Blasts in Colombo, Sri Lanka, in 2019

data.title: Alert sounded in Andhra Pradesh after Delhi blast
data.description: Police checked hotels and lodges and asked owners to alert if any strangers were moving suspiciously

data.title: Watch: 8 dead, several injured in powerful explosion near Red Fort
data.description: A high-intensity blast near Delhi’s Red Fort on November 10, 2025, killed at least eight people and left several others injured. The explosion ripped through a parked car near Lal Qila Metro Station, setting multiple vehicles ablaze and shattering windows across the area. Seven fire tenders rushed to the spot as police cordoned off the area and issued a high alert. Investigations into the cause are underway.
